summary: Decompiled a large slice of the Kraid stage file, replacing several grkraid.c placeholder functions with C implementations and recovering related config/ground-var layouts. The automated decomp.dev report showed grkraid .data becoming a new 100% match and major per-function improvements, with the PR title indicating grkraid.c reached 97%. There was no PR body and no human review feedback in the slice.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: added includes, rand_inline, a zero Vec constant, implementations for grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FE6D8, grKraid_801FE818, grKraid_801FEA00, grKraid_801FEE54, and changed grKraid_801FF150 to take a Ground_GObj*.
Recovered grKr_804D6A08_t fields and replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete Kraid function prototypes.
Expanded grKraid_GroundVars and grKraid_GroundVars2 from padding/unknown fields into offset-preserving state fields and HSD_JObj* members.

what_changed: Replaced the undecompiled marker for grKraid_801FE440 with map-rotation update logic using gv.kraid state fields, random wait time from grKr_804D6A08, grAnime_801C7FF8/grAnime_801C83D0, HSD_JObjAddRotationZ, Camera_80030E44, lb_800115F4, Ground_801C2FE0, and grKraid_801FF150(gobj).
Added static inline rand_inline(int a, int b), which picks a random value between two bounds using HSD_Randi and explicitly handles equal/zero ranges.
Added const Vec grKr_803B8278 = {0,0,0} and used it as the camera position argument in grKraid_801FE440.

Implemented grKraid_801FE6D8(HSD_JObj* hand, float param2), which reads a hand world position with lb_8000B1CC, reports "Kraid Hand Pos", computes/clamps map rotation speed from map_rot_spd_min/map_rot_spd_max/map_time_acl, triggers Ground_801C53EC(420005), and calls Camera_80030E44.

Implemented grKraid_801FE818 initialization for Kraid state: caches JObjs 31 and 47, scans grKr_804D6A08->kraid_pos_x[0..5] to identify extreme left/right slots, seeds kraid_wait_time plus optional random wait add, and initializes kraid2 state bytes/floats.

Implemented grKraid_801FEA00 as a multi-state Kraid animation/attack controller using gp->gv.kraid2.x0, animation completion checks, frame counters, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll/HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll with flag 0x10, sound/event IDs 420000 through 420008, hand attack windows, and calls to grKraid_801FE6D8.

Implemented grKraid_801FEE54 to choose a new Kraid x-position with HSD_Randi, avoid repeating the previous position when x5 is set, set translate X on the Kraid JObj and related Ground_801C2BA4(1)->hsd_obj, and derive side/direction state from kraid_pos_x.
Added includes for cm/camera.h, gr/grlib.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, lb/lb_00F9.h, and baselib/random.h to support the newly decompiled calls.
Recovered grKr_804D6A08_t from a void* unk into map_time_min, map_time_max, map_time_acl, map_rot_spd_min, map_rot_spd_max, kraid_wait_time, kraid_wait_time_add, and kraid_pos_x[6].
Updated grKraid function prototypes in grkraid.h, including grKraid_801FE6D8(HSD_JObj*, float), grKraid_801FEE54(Ground_GObj*), and grKraid_801FF150(Ground_GObj*).
Expanded grKraid_GroundVars with x10 at gp+D4 and grKraid_GroundVars2 with byte state fields x0..x5, f32 x8, s32 xC, and HSD_JObj* x10/x14.
smart_moves: Recovering grKr_804D6A08_t gave meaningful names to stage config data and coincided with the bot reporting main/melee/gr/grkraid .data as a new 100% match.
The diff keeps offset-sensitive GroundVars layouts in types.h instead of using anonymous padding, which gives the C code typed access while preserving the known gp+C4/gp+D8 layout.
The random range helper avoids calling HSD_Randi with a zero bound and handles reversed min/max inputs, matching the observed need to call rand_inline(map_time_max, map_time_min).
The Kraid position scan in grKraid_801FE818 is manually unrolled across all six kraid_pos_x entries, a common matching-friendly shape when the compiler did not emit a loop.
PAD_STACK(0x70) in grKraid_801FEA00 and PAD_STACK(0x10) in grKraid_801FEE54 show explicit stack-shape control for near matches.
The implementation preserves exact side effects likely relevant to matching and behavior: OSReport string, camera calls, animation IDs, JObj flag toggles, and Ground_801C53EC event IDs.
Updating grKraid_801FF150 from a no-argument stub to a Ground_GObj* signature aligned it with the newly decompiled caller while leaving the function body empty.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, recover the stage parameter struct early; fields like map_time_min/map_time_max/map_rot_spd_min/map_rot_spd_max/kraid_wait_time directly explain state-machine constants and make later code readable.
Ground vars can be incrementally recovered with address-offset comments and placeholder xN names; exact signedness and width of small state fields can matter for generated code.

Near-matching stage state machines often need explicit animation-completion checks, frame-counter event timing, JObj visibility flag changes, and sound/camera side effects in the same order as the original.
Use PAD_STACK when a function is logically understood but stack layout is still preventing a match.

Manual absolute-value branches, explicit casts such as (int)map->gv.kraid.x4 == 0, and literal float expressions such as 3.14159265f/180 can be more matching-friendly than cleaner library/helper abstractions.
Concrete prototypes in headers are part of decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS lets callers and callees settle on the right argument/return ABI.

A PR can be valuable even when individual functions remain below 100%; here the bot still reported grKraid_801FEA00 at 99.78%, grKraid_801FE440 at 97.82%, grKraid_801FEE54 at 99.85%, grKraid_801FE818 at 90.70%, and grKraid_801FE6D8 at 88.93%.
naming_conventions: Existing unrecovered functions keep address-based names such as grKraid_801FE440 and grKraid_801FEA00.
Stage data globals/typedefs follow the grKr_ address pattern, e.g. grKr_804D6A08 and grKr_804D6A08_t.
Recovered config fields use behavior-based names: map_time_min, map_time_max, map_time_acl, map_rot_spd_min, map_rot_spd_max, kraid_wait_time, kraid_wait_time_add, kraid_pos_x.
GroundVars fields remain provisional xN names with offset comments, e.g. x0, x1, x8, xC, x10, x14.
Local variables follow common decomp shorthand: gp for Ground*, map for the map Ground*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, and cVar/iVar/fVar-style temporaries where names were not yet semantically recovered.
No human reviewer naming feedback was present in the slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x70) in grKraid_801FEA00 and PAD_STACK(0x10) in grKraid_801FEE54.
Introduced rand_inline to reproduce bounded HSD_Randi behavior with explicit min/max/equal cases.
Stored the zero camera vector as const Vec grKr_803B8278 and copied it into a local Vec before Camera_80030E44.
Used manual absolute-value branches for floats rather than fabsf.
Used an unrolled six-entry scan of grKr_804D6A08->kraid_pos_x in grKraid_801FE818.
Kept exact event/frame constants such as 80, 45, 55, 123..138, 147..169, 161, 246, and event IDs 420000-420008.
Used HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll and HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll with flag 0x10 on both the current Kraid JObj and Ground_801C2BA4(1)->hsd_obj.
Used explicit decrement style gp->gv.kraid.x10 += -1 and gp->gv.kraid2.xC += -1 in places instead of cleaner -- syntax.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched data for GALE01 increased to 37.56% (+0.03%, +304 bytes). main/melee/gr/grkraid .data became a new 100.00% match, +13 bytes.
No human review comments or reviews were present; PR body was empty.

follow_up_queries: grKraid_801FE6D8 88.93% map_rot_spd formula handpos OSReport Kraid Hand Pos
grKraid_801FE818 90.70% kraid_pos_x unrolled min max scan
grKraid_801FEE54 99.85% HSD_Randi position selection x3 x4 x5
grKraid_GroundVars2 signedness x0 x1 x2 x3 x4 x5 x8 xC x10 x14
grKraid_801FF150 Ground_GObj parameter empty stub later callers
